title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You recently attended a fitness class at your local sports centre. Unfortunately, you were very dissatisfied with the experience. Write a letter to the manager of the sports centre describing what happened, explaining why you were disappointed, and asking for a refund.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Manager,

I am writing to complain about the fitness class I attended at your sports centre last Saturday morning. I paid for the 'Advanced Yoga' session at 10 a.m., but my experience was deeply unsatisfactory.

Firstly, the instructor arrived fifteen minutes late without any explanation or apology. This shortened the class considerably, meaning I did not receive the full hour I had paid for. Furthermore, the room was uncomfortably warm and the ventilation system appeared to be broken. Several participants left early because of the heat, which speaks for itself.

To make matters worse, the instructor was not teaching the advertised 'Advanced' class. The exercises were clearly designed for complete beginners, which was frustrating for those of us with previous experience.

I believe it is only fair that I receive a full refund for this class, given that it failed to meet even basic standards. I would also strongly recommend addressing these issues before offering future sessions.

I look forward to your prompt reply.

Yours faithfully,
Daniel Brooks

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school has asked you to write a report about sports and physical activity among students. Your report should describe how active students currently are, explain why exercise is important for young people, and suggest ways the school could encourage more physical activity.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Physical Activity Among Students at Lakeview School

Introduction
This report examines the current levels of physical activity among students at Lakeview School and offers recommendations for improvement.

Current Levels of Activity
A recent survey of 200 students revealed that only 35% exercise for the recommended 60 minutes per day. The majority of students spend their free time on screens rather than engaging in sports or outdoor activities. Participation in after-school sports clubs has also declined by 20% in the last two years.

Why Exercise Matters
Regular physical activity is essential for both physical and mental health. It helps maintain a healthy weight, strengthens bones, and reduces stress and anxiety — all particularly important during the demanding teenage years.

Recommendations
The school could introduce compulsory lunchtime activity sessions twice a week. Additionally, offering a wider variety of sports, such as dance, martial arts, or yoga, might appeal to students who dislike traditional team games. Finally, a school-wide 'Step Challenge' using smartphone apps could motivate students to be more active.

Conclusion
Encouraging exercise at school will benefit students' health and academic performance alike.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in a health and fitness magazine:

'We are looking for reviews of sports centres, gyms, or fitness classes in your area. Tell us about the facilities, the staff, and whether you would recommend it to others.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Review: FitZone Sports Centre — Is It Worth It?

FitZone opened in our town six months ago and has quickly become the most talked-about fitness facility in the area. Having visited several times, I feel well-placed to give an honest assessment.

The facilities are undoubtedly impressive. The gym is spacious, well-lit, and equipped with the latest machines. There is also a heated swimming pool, a yoga studio, and a dedicated cycling room. Everything is kept immaculately clean, which is a significant plus.

The staff are friendly and knowledgeable. Personal trainers are available without an appointment for quick advice, and there is always someone nearby if you need help with equipment.

However, FitZone does have one notable weakness: the cost. Monthly membership fees are considerably higher than other local gyms, and group classes require an additional charge on top of this. For students on a budget, this is a real barrier.

Overall, FitZone is an excellent facility if you can afford it. For those looking for premium equipment and professional guidance, it is absolutely worth the investment.
